title: Current Account Services (CAS) Online
body_text:
Current Account Services (CAS) Online
1	Introduction to CAS Online
1.1	Overview of CAS Online
Current Account Services (CAS) Online allows financial institutions to view their Central 1 account information in near real-time. Account information is available for each currency type the financial institution maintains with Central 1 (i.e., Canadian dollars, US dollars, Pound Sterling, and Euros). Data is refreshed every 20 to 30 minutes throughout the Business Day. Opening balances, which include overnight settlement transactions, are generally available on CAS Online by 5:30 am PT (8:30 am ET). Transaction history is available on CAS Online for one year.
Depending on the financial institutionâs preference, CAS Online can be set up with one settlement account or with multiple settlement accounts. Refer to Chapter 6, Using the Account Summary Function, for more information.
Related Information
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
CPS Admin Application Part
1.2	Central 1 Account Interest Calculations
This section describes how deposit, loan, and overdraft interest on Central 1 accounts is calculated and paid, and how float charges are calculated on foreign currency accounts.
Central 1 account deposit interest is only for Canadian and US dollar accounts. It is calculated over 365 days and is paid once per month at month-end. The Canadian dollar interest is based on the account credit balance while the US dollar interest is based on the excess float amount.
Float charges apply to US dollar Central 1 accounts. These float charges reflect the difference in timing between when Central 1 settles with member financial institutions and when Central 1 receives settlement from agent banks and other financial institutions.
A float factor will be applied to the accumulated value of deposit and currency return items only. This is then divided by the number of days in the month in order to calculate the average daily float requirement. The calculated float is offset by the sum of the daily netted credit balances in the accounts. If the average credit balance in the account falls below the float requirement and causes a float deficiency, interest charges are applied.
For US dollar accounts, monthly float charges are applied to cheques payable in US dollars that are deposited or returned by the financial institution based upon a float factor of 2.45
days. Monthly float charges are also applied to US currency deposits (returns) based upon a float factor of 1.0 day.
Central 1 account loan/overdraft interest is for Canadian and US dollar accounts. It is calculated over 365 days and is charged once per month at month-end. The Canadian dollar interest is based on the account debit balance and line of credit limits. The same calculation applies to US debit balances.
1.3	Backdating Transactions
Backdating transactions to the previous statement period can only be done on the first Business Day of the month and prior to the close of the previous month-end cycle.
Backdated transactions appear on the Central 1 account statement and on CAS Online in settlement date order.
